title: PROP-FEED-S5-019 (EC-FEED-019 double-click race) does not actually exercise a race; it asserts a Sprint-1 invariant that was already covered
description:
Verification arch §14 PROP-FEED-S5-019 specifies: '1 回目クリックで editingStatus === "switching" に遷移、pendingNextFocus が設定された状態で 2 回目クリックを発火。… mock adapter の dispatchSelectPastNote が 2 回目はカウントされない (call count = 1)'.

The implemented test (FeedRow.dom.vitest.ts lines 842-861) does NOT perform the first click. It synthesizes a switching-state viewState directly, dispatches one click, and asserts dispatchSelectPastNote was not called. There is no transition from idle/editing → switching driven by a real first click. The test therefore cannot detect a regression where (a) the first click is incorrectly debounced, (b) the second click happens during the synchronous re-render after the first click but before the state update commits, or (c) feedReducer accidentally permits the second click during the brief 'editing' → 'switching' transition.

The test as written is functionally equivalent to existing Sprint 1 PROP-FEED-013 (which already verifies REQ-FEED-006 click suppression) and provides no new coverage for the EC-FEED-019 race scenario the spec defined.
expectedBehavior: A test that mounts in `editing` state, clicks once (asserts dispatchSelectPastNote called once), simulates the reducer transitioning to `switching` (or asserts the channel-driven transition), then clicks again on the same row and asserts dispatchSelectPastNote.mock.calls.length === 1.
actualBehavior: Single-click test against pre-set switching state; no race condition exercised.
